title: Mastery Exam: CSS Grid and Debugging
summary: Mastery Exam: CSS Grid and Debugging retrieves prior skills in an original independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply grid formatting context, grid track sizing, grid placement, grid areas, grid auto fit fill, grid alignment, grid subgrid, css debug method in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 1: only explicitly declared cells can participate in Grid layout.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 1: inspect explicit tracks, implicit tracks, direct items, and auto-placement before deciding whether the layout contract is two-dimensional.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 1: declare a cell for every possible item because Grid cannot generate tracks.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 3: explicit visual placement changes reading and keyboard focus order.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 3: preserve meaningful DOM order, place items against named lines where useful, and avoid dense or arbitrary visual reordering for task sequences.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 3: use grid placement to move the most important interactive item first for every input mode.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 4: one area name can form disconnected or L-shaped regions in a valid template.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 4: use rectangular semantic areas, redefine the complete template at real content breakpoints, and keep source order independent of the visual map.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 4: name areas left blue and bottom so the template mirrors the current screenshot.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 5: auto-fit and auto-fill always create identical empty-track behavior.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 5: choose auto-fit when occupied tracks should expand after empty tracks collapse, or auto-fill when the empty track structure should remain.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 5: use auto-fit for a fixed number of columns because it guarantees exactly three tracks.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 6: align-items positions the entire grid inside its container.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 6: identify whether tracks, all items, or one item must move, then choose content, items, or self alignment on the logical axis.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 6: use physical left and right assumptions because Grid alignment axes never change with writing mode.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 8: trying random property changes is faster than isolating the smallest failing case.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 8: reproduce precisely, form one falsifiable hypothesis, inspect browser evidence, make a bounded repair, and rerun a named regression matrix.
Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 8: rewrite the component when computed styles differ from expectations instead of tracing the cascade.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 26: one fractional unit always equals one fixed fraction of viewport width.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 26: set overflow hidden on the grid when an auto minimum makes a track wider than expected.

options.text: Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 29: auto-fit and auto-fill always create identical empty-track behavior.

Independent Grid algorithm source-order and root-cause decisions case 29: choose auto-fit when occupied tracks should expand after empty tracks collapse, or auto-fill when the empty track structure should remain.
